title: athanasia pubescens
intro: Athanasia pubescens, also known as Tanacetum parthenium or Feverfew, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Athanasia pubescens include relief from headaches, fever reduction, and the prevention of migraine attacks, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps. Athanasia pubescens can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and dried flowers, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
